description: Raw server HTML uses POSITIONAL <td> (rows have data-row_id, no ninja_clmn_nm_* classes -- those are JS-added); rendered DOM has the classes. Make extraction handle both: support positional selectors (tr[data-row_id] + td:nth-child(n)) for the HTTP path and class selectors for the browser path, via scrape_config. For OCWD: title=td1, sol=td2, posted=td4, due=td5. status/days are JS-computed (empty in raw) -> derive open/closed by filtering on due date in code, don't rely on JS.
title: Selector extraction tolerant of raw-HTML vs rendered DOM
